---
title: "Computational musicology"
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: journal
---

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
  var target = "#overview";
  if (window.location.hash !== target) {
    window.location.hash = target;
  }
});
</script>

# Overview

This repo contains materials related to computational musicology course. To be more specific, the content of breakout sessions ( = classworks) and some draft material for future final portfolio.

The object of study of later is [**Radiohead - Knives Out (2001)**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2Lpw3yMCWro&list=RD2Lpw3yMCWro). Several operations have been performed. In particular, two temporal features: *(1)* **a chromatic analysis**, in which pitch information was charted in time; *(2)* **a timbre analysis**, which charts mel-frequencies information in time. The other part covers temporal self-similarity by utilizing **self-similarity matrices** for *(3)* **chromatic** features, and *(4)* **timbre-based** features.

# Radiohead

## Analysis Views {.tabset}

### Chromatic + Timbre (week 8)

<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px;"> 
  <img src="images/radiohead_chroma.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin chromagram"> 
  <img src="images/radiohead_timbre.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin cepstrogram"> 
</div>

(1) *G* is consistently strong that suggests it's being the tonal center.

### Self-Similarity Matrices (week 9)

<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px;"> 
  <img src="images/radiohead_ssm_pitch.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin chromagram"> 
  <img src="images/radiohead_ssm_timbre.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin cepstrogram"> 
</div>

(1) the diagonal lines are strong, suggesting quite similar, recurring sections. 
(2) the checkerboard pattern is particularly present, suggesting an abundance of recurring sections.

### Keygrams + Chordograms (week 10)

<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px;"> 
  <img src="images/radiohead_keygram.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin chromagram"> 
  <img src="images/radiohead_chordogram.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin cepstrogram"> 
</div>

(1) the keygram centers around G# min and Bb min, suggesting this window being the tonal center.
(2) certain patterns can be noticed. for instance, the c#min-centered tonal information is alternating for the whole track. at the same time, the F#min takes the lead during the other part of the phase. this information can provide insight for further chord-progression analysis.

# Classwork

## Classroom Views {.tabset}

### Aphex Twin + Chroma (Week 8)

<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px;"> 
  <img src="images/aphex_chroma_v1.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin chromagram"> 
  <img src="images/aphex_chroma_sst_v1.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin cepstrogram"> 
</div>

### Aphex Twin + Timbre (Week 9)

<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px;"> 
  <img src="images/aphex_timbre_v1.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin chromagram"> 
  <img src="images/aphex_timbre_sst_v1.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin cepstrogram"> 
</div>

### Zager and Evans’ Keygram + Chordogram (Week 10)

<div style="display: flex; gap: 20px;">     
  <img src="images/twnety_five_keygram.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin chromagram"> 
  <img src="images/twnety_five_chordogram.png" width="400" alt="aphex twin cepstrogram"> 
</div>
